raw_consumer_statement: I purchased a chandelier for {$1600.00} from Designer Lighting ( XXXX NJ ) in XX/XX/year>. It arrived damaged. The merchant agreed to refund me if I returned the item. They promised to send me a return mailing label many times but 4 months later and they have not sent it. I filed a billing dispute with Citibank Master card. After 4 months Citibank said although my proof was more than sufficient to justify a refund the merchant refused to issue a refund. Doesn't Citibank have means to collect that from the merchant? Arbitration? Escalation to Mastercard? Just because the merchant is a fraud and will not issue a refund ( even though they did say in writing they would refund me multiple times ) does Citibank just accept that and not get my money back? Doesn't Citibank have other means to get that refund to me? I would think many merchants would simply refuse to refund for a billing dispute if they never had to refund money.
